hypothesis: Quantitative task-tool embedding similarity thresholds exist that predict whether fine-tuning a domain-specific foundation model will outperform tool augmentation of a general-purpose model, such that when the mean minimum cosine distance between task embeddings and available tool embeddings exceeds a learnable critical value (estimated around 0.65 based on the Spectra environment results), investing in domain-specific model training will yield greater performance gains per unit of development cost than designing additional aligned tools.
